sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a history of injuring his right knee while engaging in trampoline activities a week ago, which was characterized by a sudden onset of pain and a "pop" sensation.
He reports that the pain is localized to the lateral aspect of the knee.
He has been taking Tylenol for the pain, but it has not provided significant relief.
The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes, for which he is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily and metformin 500mg daily.
He reports that he has not been consistent with his medication regimen, particularly with his blood pressure medication.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, diabetes, and is not consistent with his medication regimen.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 180/80
Pulse rate: [NOT MENTIONED]
Oxygen saturation: [NOT MENTIONED]
Temperature: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's blood pressure was noted to be elevated.
The heart had a regular rate and rhythm without any murmurs.
The lungs were clear bilaterally.
The knee examination revealed ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect, but no effusion.
The patient had a slight limp and was able to bear weight, but had pain with some movement.
Test Results:
The patient's right knee x-ray showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain of the right knee and hypertension that requires further management.
Problem list:
Right knee pain; Lateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; Diabetes.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for the pain and swelling of the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I continued the lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension and ordered an echocardiogram to assess cardiac function.
I also ordered an A1c to assess the patient's long-term blood sugar control and a lipid panel to assess lipid profiles.
Follow-up:
I will see the patient again after the results of the ordered tests are available to discuss further management.
